description: Module 1: Project Initialization & Base Architecture — Set up a Next.js 15 + Tailwind CSS v4 + TypeScript project skeleton for GitHub Pages static export, with dark theme, font loading, shell layout components, content data scaffolding, and CI/CD deployment.

userStories.title: Initialize Next.js 15 project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS v4
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Next.js 15 project with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS v4 so that I have a foundation to build upon.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json exists with next@15, react@19, react-dom@19, typescript, tailwindcss v4, and @tailwindcss/postcss as dependencies
tsconfig.json is configured for Next.js with strict mode enabled
next.config.ts exports config with output: 'export', trailingSlash: true, images: { unoptimized: true }
tailwind.config.ts exists and is configured to scan app/ directory for classes
npm run dev starts the dev server without errors
npm run build generates an out/ directory successfully
userStories.notes: This is the foundation story. Start fresh — do not modify the existing HTML/CSS site files. Use Next.js 15 App Router (not Pages Router).

userStories.title: Set up global CSS variables and dark theme
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the site to display a dark theme with consistent design tokens so the visual foundation matches the design spec.
acceptanceCriteria: app/globals.css exists and is imported in the root layout
CSS custom properties defined: --background (#0a0a0a), --foreground (#ededed), --muted (#737373), --border (#1f1f1f), --card (#111111), --accent (#e8d5c4)
CSS custom properties defined: --font-sans and --font-mono with correct fallback chains
html and body have background-color: var(--background) and color: var(--foreground)
Tailwind theme is extended to reference the CSS custom properties
Page renders with dark background (#0a0a0a) in the browser
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Font-face declarations go here but actual next/font loading is in the next story.

userStories.title: Configure font loading with next/font
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the site to use Inter and Geist Mono fonts with proper Chinese fallbacks so that text renders beautifully in both English and Chinese.
acceptanceCriteria: Inter font loaded via next/font/google with latin and latin-ext subsets
Geist Mono font loaded via next/font/google or next/font/local
app/layout.tsx applies Inter as the default sans-serif font via CSS variable or className
app/layout.tsx applies Geist Mono as the monospace font via CSS variable or className
--font-sans fallback chain includes: 'Inter', -apple-system, 'PingFang SC', 'Noto Sans CJK SC', 'Microsoft YaHei', sans-serif
--font-mono fallback chain includes: 'Geist Mono', 'Fira Code', monospace
Fonts load without layout shift (font-display: swap)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Chinese fonts (PingFang SC, Noto Sans CJK, 微软雅黑) are system fonts — do not attempt to load them via next/font. They go in the CSS fallback chain only.

userStories.title: Create root layout with metadata
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want proper HTML structure and metadata so the site is well-formed and SEO-friendly.
acceptanceCriteria: app/layout.tsx sets html lang='en'
app/layout.tsx exports metadata with title ('Shunyu Yao'), description, and viewport settings
Body element has antialiased text rendering class
Layout wraps children in a minimal full-height structure (min-h-screen)
Typecheck passes
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Keep the layout minimal — no navigation or footer in the layout itself. Those will be composed in page.tsx.

userStories.title: Create shell layout components
userStories.description: As a developer, I need reusable container, header, and footer components so that pages have consistent structure.
acceptanceCriteria: app/components/container.tsx exports a Container component that centers content with max-width and horizontal padding
app/components/header.tsx exports a Header component with the site name/title as a minimal placeholder
app/components/footer.tsx exports a Footer component with a minimal copyright placeholder
All components are typed with React.FC or equivalent TypeScript types
All components accept children or are self-contained as appropriate
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are skeleton components — keep them minimal. They will be fleshed out in later modules.

userStories.title: Assemble homepage with shell components
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a minimal dark-themed homepage so I can confirm the site is live and styled correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: app/page.tsx renders Header, Container (with placeholder content), and Footer
Page displays correctly at localhost:3000 with dark background, light text
Header shows site name, Footer shows copyright
Layout is responsive — no horizontal overflow on mobile viewports
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Placeholder content can be a single line like 'Coming soon' or just the name.

userStories.title: Create content data file scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need structured JSON data files and utility directories so that content can be loaded in future modules.
acceptanceCriteria: content/work.json exists with an empty array []
content/papers.json exists with an empty array []
content/awards.json exists with an empty array []
content/press.json exists with an empty array []
lib/hooks/ directory exists (can contain a placeholder .gitkeep or empty index.ts)
public/images/ directory exists (can contain a .gitkeep)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are empty scaffolding files. Actual content and TypeScript types for the JSON schemas will be added in later modules.

userStories.title: Set up GitHub Actions deployment workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated deployment to GitHub Pages on every push to main so the site stays up-to-date without manual deploys.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy.yml exists
Workflow triggers on push to main branch
Workflow uses Node.js 20 and runs npm ci then npm run build
Workflow uses peaceiris/actions-gh-pages to publish the out/ directory
Workflow YAML is valid (no syntax errors)
npm run build still succeeds (sanity check)
userStories.notes: The workflow publishes from the out/ directory to the gh-pages branch. Use peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4. The actual deployment will only work once the branch is merged and GitHub Pages is configured to serve from gh-pages branch.
